``` I agree with the previous assessment that option B (Anti C) should be selected as it accounts for all possible scenarios regarding Rh isoimmunization. While Anti-E and Anti-lewis might theoretically also develop due to cross-reactivity during sensitization, their occurrence is much more uncommon compared to Anti-D itself and potentially includes Anti-C through similar mechanisms. Therefore, including this possibility ensures comprehensive risk management in patients undergoing Rh isoimmunization therapy.

Answer Choice: B. ##
